title: podman Failed to Install Ubuntu 20.04
body:
/kind bug

**Description**


<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.  Follow the instruction from the installation page on podman.io
     * https://podman.io/getting-started/installation

2.  use `apt install` command:

```
 $ sudo apt -y install podman
Reading package lists... Done
Building dependency tree       
Reading state information... Done
E: Unable to locate package podman
```

3.  Double checking ...

```
 $ apt search podman
Sorting... Done
Full Text Search... Done
conmon/focal 2.0.9-1 amd64
  OCI container runtime monitor

resource-agents/focal 1:4.5.0-2ubuntu2 amd64
  Cluster Resource Agents
```
   *  Something to note, the label: "conmon/" above.  
   * Is that a typo in the configuration?  That line is pasted directly from the termina.


**Describe the results you received:**

* Error: "`E: Unable to locate package podman`"
* Can't use or run podman

As an aside, these machines are unable to use Snaps because Snap prevents programs running when there are symbolic links involved.  So we cannot use that either.

**Describe the results you expected:**

* Expected podman to install so that I can get going on my project.
* I doubt that Docker will be enough 

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

This was reported earlier in  

*  [Failed to install podman with download.opensuse.org repo on Travis Ubuntu bionic non x86_64](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/5447) (5447)

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

*  N/A can not install

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

*  N/A can not install
*  Podman snap unusable due to symlink bugs
    * https://bugs.launchpad.net/snapd/+bug/1886113
    *  https://forum.snapcraft.io/t/how-can-i-use-snap-when-i-dont-use-home-user/3352/11


**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

* Physical AMD x86_64
* Ubuntu 20.04 (all packages up to date).

comments.body:
/update

I believe the problem lies behind the keyboard.  However the documentation for Ubuntu on the install page seems to omit a key step.

* https://podman.io/getting-started/installation#Ubuntu

Therein it has:
```
  :
curl -L https: ...
sudo apt-get update -qq
sudo apt-get -qq -y install podman
```
Which wasn't working for me until I did an `apt upgrade` then I was able to install `podman`

```
sudo apt update
sudo apt upgrade
sudo apt install podman
```

Personally I really don't want "quiet" when I'm loading new untried packages.


Yah!! The correct outcome appears to have occured ...


```
 $ podman info --debug

  arch: amd64

  swapFree: 2081693696
```

In summary there's one more step needed, it seems.
Would you mind making a PR to update the install page with this? It lives at https://github.com/containers/podman.io/blob/master/getting-started/installation.md
